cards.question: State the formula and charge of: sulfate, nitrate, carbonate.
cards.answer: Sulfate SOв‚„ВІвЃ», nitrate NOв‚ѓвЃ», carbonate COв‚ѓВІвЃ».
cards.explanation: These polyatomic ions must be memorised вЂ” they appear constantly in formulae and equations.

cards.question: State the formula and charge of: ammonium, hydroxide, phosphate.
cards.answer: Ammonium NHв‚„вЃє, hydroxide OHвЃ», phosphate POв‚„ВівЃ».
cards.explanation: Ammonium is the common positive polyatomic ion; phosphate carries 3в€’.

cards.question: Deduce the formula of the compound formed between magnesium ions (MgВІвЃє) and nitrate ions (NOв‚ѓвЃ»).
cards.explanation: Charges must balance: one MgВІвЃє needs two NOв‚ѓвЃ». Bracket the whole polyatomic ion before the subscript.

cards.question: Deduce the formulae of (i) aluminium oxide and (ii) ammonium carbonate.
cards.explanation: Cross over the charges: AlВівЃє and OВІвЃ» в†’ Alв‚‚Oв‚ѓ; two NHв‚„вЃє balance one COв‚ѓВІвЃ».

cards.question: A compound is named iron(III) sulfate. Deduce its formula, using the oxidation number in the name.
cards.answer: Iron(III) means FeВівЃє; sulfate is SOв‚„ВІвЃ». Balancing charges gives Feв‚‚(SOв‚„)в‚ѓ.
cards.explanation: The Roman numeral gives the metal's oxidation number/charge; then balance with the anion.

cards.question: Name the compound CuSOв‚„ using oxidation number nomenclature. [Cu is +2]
cards.explanation: Transition metals need the oxidation number in brackets because they form more than one ion.
